summary: One-click launcher uses GET /health on port 7712 as the idempotency gate. If daemon healthy: warm path (skip startup, re-open browser only). If not healthy: cold path (load env, start daemon, start Vite, open browser). Warm path completes in ~3-7s; cold path in ~12-15s.
title: Start-PawnPortal.ps1 Idempotency Pattern

summary: Using System.Diagnostics.ProcessStartInfo with UseShellExecute=false ensures PPLX_API_KEY (set via [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('Process')) is inherited by the daemon subprocess. Start-Process default (UseShellExecute=true) may NOT inherit modified env vars on all Windows versions. Always use UseShellExecute=false when child must inherit parent's modified env.
title: ProcessStartInfo UseShellExecute=false for Env Inheritance

summary: SDS.env canonical name is PERPLEXITY_API_KEY. daemon_wrapper.py reads PPLX_API_KEY. The launcher bridges these at load time: if PPLX_API_KEY empty and PERPLEXITY_API_KEY set, alias it. This pattern keeps the secret in one canonical SDS.env location while remaining compatible with code that uses either name.
title: PERPLEXITY_API_KEY vs PPLX_API_KEY Aliasing

summary: PowerShell 5.1 reads .ps1 files as Windows-1252 (not UTF-8) when no BOM is present. Multi-byte UTF-8 sequences for em dash (U+2014: 0xE2 0x80 0x94) produce 0x94 which cp1252 maps to RIGHT DOUBLE QUOTATION MARK (U+201D). PS5.1 treats curly quotes as string delimiters, causing 'missing terminator' parse errors. Fix: ASCII-only in PS1 scripts. Checkmarks -> [OK], em dashes -> --.
title: ASCII-Only Rule for PS5.1 Script Files

summary: In browser mode (Vite dev server, no Electron), HelmSettings comes from React default state -- not from %APPDATA%/helm-settings.json. So settings.modules is always {}. To enable Pawn in browser mode, enabledByDefault must be true in registry.ts. Changed from false to true in K510. Electron mode can override via helm-settings.json; launcher also patches that file for belt-and-suspenders.
title: Pawn Module enabledByDefault:true for Browser Mode

summary: K510 chose: daemon started directly (Python) + Vite dev server for UI, rather than launching the Electron app which manages daemon internally. Rationale: (1) browser-native URL (localhost:5173) works with -Browser parameter; (2) no Electron overhead; (3) daemon and Vite can be started/stopped independently; (4) avoids double-daemon risk if Electron is also running. Tradeoff: no Electron supervision loop (crash restart). Acceptable for K510 scope.
title: Daemon-as-Standalone vs Electron-Managed Architecture

summary: Start-PawnPortal.ps1 starts the Vite dev server immediately after launching the daemon (before the health poll loop). Both start concurrently. Vite typically ready in 3-4s; daemon health in 4-6s. Opening browser after daemon health check ensures /pawn endpoint is ready when user submits first query. Total cold-start improvement vs sequential: ~3-4s saved.
title: Vite Started in Parallel with Daemon Health Wait

summary: Stop-PawnPortal.ps1 finds daemon processes by port (7711 MCP + 7712 REST) via Get-NetTCPConnection -State Listen. This is process-name-agnostic -- works whether daemon is python, python3, python3.13, etc. Belt-and-suspenders: checks both ports since daemon may have started but REST not yet bound. Also kills Vite on 5173.
title: Stop-PawnPortal.ps1 Port-Based Process Detection

summary: The workspace .gitignore has a blanket *.ps1 rule (line 241). Adding launcher files required explicit negation exceptions: !librarian-mcp-helm-pwa/Start-PawnPortal.ps1 and !librarian-mcp-helm-pwa/Stop-PawnPortal.ps1. Pattern: blanket-ignore + targeted negation. Verified via git status showing files as untracked (not ignored). See also TS-036 (K506) for prior gitignore carve-out pattern.
title: BRIDLE Step-0: *.ps1 Gitignore Carve-Out Pattern

summary: Live C.3 test: sonar-pro model (April 2026) identifies the _AUTHORITY_HEADER substrate-injection wrapper as an adversarial jailbreak attempt and refuses to use it. This is a vendor-model policy change since K509/R12. The Layer-2 architecture (enrichment + API call) works mechanically -- substrate is delivered. Model response behavior is vendor-side. Does NOT block K510 success criteria (chips metadata, endpoint health, enrichment chain all verified). See TS-041/TS-043 for prior art.
title: sonar-pro Authority-Wrapper Resistance (April 2026)

summary: PowerShell .ps1 files cannot be pinned directly to Windows taskbar (shell restriction). Workaround: create a .cmd shim: @echo off / powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -WindowStyle Hidden -File %~dp0Start-PawnPortal.ps1 / exit. Pin the .cmd file. This is a one-time manual step documented in README and Knight report. K510 explicitly excluded programmatic .lnk creation.
title: Taskbar Pinning via .cmd Shim

summary: K510 Phase D added the canonical Layer-2 bring-up section to VENDOR_SHUTDOWN_RUNBOOK_B125.md: one-click command (Start-PawnPortal.ps1) + manual fallback (load env, start Python daemon, start Vite, health check). This closes the runbook gap where Layer-2 migration steps were documented (swap vendor API key) but the initial bring-up was not.
title: Layer-2 Bring-Up Now Documented in VENDOR_SHUTDOWN_RUNBOOK
